profile.name: Jirathiwat Suntipreedatham
profile.headline: Software engineer and part-time IT CITY sales consultant building real-time AI systems, cinematic recommendation engines, and production-ready web applications.
profile.bio: Hi to see you there! I am Jirathiwat Suntipreedatham, a software engineer, Faculty of Information and Communication Technology, Mahidol University student, and part-time Sales Consultant at IT CITY Public Company Limited in Bangkok. My background includes international computer competition experience, international seminar experience, leadership in an International Relations Program, project work, sales work, and marketing department experience.

posts.title: Apple WWDC26: Siri AI, Apple Intelligence, and the Developer Platform Shift
posts.summary: Apple's latest WWDC push puts Siri AI, Apple Intelligence, stronger parental controls, and platform-wide software upgrades at the center of its next developer cycle.
posts.fullDescription:
Apple's WWDC26 announcement is important because it shows where the company wants the next software cycle to go: more AI inside everyday workflows, a stronger Siri AI experience, platform-wide improvements across iOS, iPadOS, macOS, watchOS, visionOS, and tvOS, and more attention on safety and family controls.

For developers, the interesting part is not only the headline AI branding. The real question is how deeply Apple will let developers connect their apps into intelligent system experiences. If Siri AI becomes more capable and Apple Intelligence becomes more useful across apps, the value of clean app architecture, privacy-first design, and native platform integration becomes even higher.

My take: this is Apple trying to make AI feel less like a separate chatbot and more like a system layer. That direction fits Apple's strengths, but developers will need clear APIs, strong tooling, and predictable behavior before it becomes a real productivity jump.

Sources: Apple Newsroom WWDC26 announcement and Apple Developer WWDC26 video.

posts.title: IMAX Theater Review: Why Premium Cinema Still Feels Like Future Tech
posts.summary: IMAX is expanding new laser locations while immersive venues like Cosm attract major investment, showing that the theater experience is becoming a technology product again.
posts.fullDescription:
A good IMAX theater is still one of the clearest examples of technology changing how content feels. The screen scale, projection brightness, image stability, and tuned audio system make the movie feel less like a file being played and more like a physical event.

The current theater market is interesting because premium formats are growing while ordinary cinema has to fight harder for attention. IMAX has announced new IMAX with Laser locations in 2026, including expansion partnerships in India and the southeastern United States. At the same time, immersive shared-reality venues such as Cosm are attracting major entertainment investment, which suggests that audiences still want large-scale experiences when the technology is special enough.

My review angle: IMAX is strongest when the movie is designed for it. For large-format sci-fi, action, space, or practical-camera spectacle, the format adds real value. For smaller dialogue-heavy films, the benefit is less dramatic. The future of theaters will likely depend on this kind of premium differentiation: bigger screens, better sound, higher brightness, and experiences that cannot be recreated on a laptop.

Sources: IMAX investor news on 2026 IMAX with Laser expansion, IMAX official theater experience information, and recent reporting on Sony's investment in Cosm.

posts.title: Building Real-Time AI With Operating-System Thinking
posts.summary: A technical note on why real-time computer vision is not only about model accuracy, but also memory flow, scheduling, synchronization, and predictable execution.
posts.fullDescription:
Real-time AI systems fail when the software around the model cannot keep up with the model itself. In the Iron Dome Missile Tracker project, the core challenge was not simply detecting objects in video. The system also needed stable frame handling, low-latency inference, and predictable write-backs while many tasks were competing for resources.

This post highlights the engineering mindset behind that work: treat computer vision as a complete system. Memory management, thread coordination, queue design, and I/O behavior all affect how intelligent software behaves under pressure.

The result is a portfolio case study that connects AI, operating systems, and software engineering into one production-style build.

posts.title: CineMatch 2.0 and the Search for Movie Vibe
posts.summary: A behind-the-scenes look at semantic movie search, hybrid scoring, hidden-gem discovery, and why recommendation systems should understand intent instead of only keywords.
posts.fullDescription:
CineMatch 2.0 was designed around a simple idea: people do not always search for movies by title, actor, or genre. Sometimes they search by mood, visual style, emotional impact, or narrative complexity.

The platform uses metadata soup, semantic embeddings, cosine similarity, and weighted statistical quality to recommend films that match the user intent. It also includes a Hidden Gem Radar to reduce blockbuster bias and surface interesting films that might otherwise be buried.

For me, this project is a strong example of how data science can become a real product experience when the model, ranking logic, and interface all work together.

posts.title: Evolving This Portfolio Into an Editable Vercel Web App
posts.summary: How this portfolio moved from a static website idea into a full editable portfolio with admin controls, posts, certificates, media uploads, and Vercel-ready backend support.
posts.fullDescription:
This portfolio is built to be more than a profile page. It is a content system for projects, experience, certificates, skills, blog posts, images, and embedded videos.

The backend/admin panel makes it possible to edit portfolio information without changing code every time. Since the site is now prepared for Vercel, it can use backend routes and storage patterns that were not available in a GitHub Pages-only deployment.

The goal is to make the portfolio feel alive: animated, editable, visual, and ready to grow with each new project.

projects.name: Iron Dome Missile Tracker v3.1
projects.description: A high-performance real-time tactical computer vision application that optimizes resource-heavy AI inference pipelines inside bounded, time-critical software environments. The system demonstrates how custom OS resource management can accelerate machine learning workloads while handling HD video processing, deep learning inference, and telemetry write-backs without dropping frames.
projects.associated: Faculty of Information and Communication Technology, Mahidol University
highlights: Perfect ITCS225 grading rubric score of 16/16
Deterministic execution with ultra-low latency and frame-rate stability
projects.fullDescription:
The Iron Dome Missile Tracker v3.1 is a production-ready system demonstrating practical applications of Operating Systems concepts in a high-performance real-time environment. Built as a capstone-level project for the ITCS225 course, it achieved a perfect 16/16 grading rubric score. The system seamlessly integrates four major OS components—Synchronization, Memory Management, Task Scheduling, and File I/O—into a functional object detection pipeline that sustains 60fps performance without dropping frames.

[media: 2]

Powered by a custom-trained YOLOv8 AI engine fine-tuned on a curated Roboflow dataset of over 9,200 missile images, the tracker handles high-definition video processing, deep learning inference, and telemetry write-backs simultaneously. 

[media: 3]



To achieve this deterministic execution, the architecture utilizes custom system-level memory management, strict thread scheduling, and advanced synchronization techniques. The application is cross-platform, offering native CPU tracking on all operating systems alongside GPU-accelerated CUDA support on Windows and Metal support on macOS.

[media: 5]

projects.name: CineMatch 2.0: The Cinematic Oracle
projects.description: A production-grade movie discovery platform for ITCS227 that uses GPU-accelerated semantic search, high-dimensional embeddings, and hybrid recommendation logic to understand the soul and vibe of more than 436,000 movies.
projects.associated: Faculty of Information and Communication Technology, Mahidol University
highlights: 50/50 hybrid score combining semantic similarity and IMDB weighted quality
Mood Fusion intent parser for visual style
Hidden Gem Radar and interactive Plotly 3D discovery explorer
projects.fullDescription:
CineMatch 2.0 is a production-grade movie discovery platform designed for the ITCS227 Data Science course. It transcends simple keyword matching by understanding the 'Soul' and 'Vibe' of over 436,000 movies using high-dimensional embeddings and a custom Hybrid Scoring Engine. 

[media: 3]

The platform features an advanced architecture encompassing data engineering (processing datasets into a 'Metadata Soup' fingerprint using Title, Overview, Genre, Keywords, and Era Tags), an AI Core utilizing the all-MiniLM-L6-v2 model for GPU-accelerated vectorization, and a modern Streamlit web interface.\n\nUnlike standard search engines, CineMatch uses a 50/50 Hybrid Score. 

[media: 4]



It combines Semantic Similarity—measured using Cosine Similarity of query embeddings—with a Weighted Statistical Quality based on the IMDB rating formula. The system also features a Mood Fusion Intent Parser that automatically detects if a query matches Visual Styles (e.g., Neon, Noir), Emotional Impacts, or Narrative Complexity to adjust scoring weights on the fly. Finally, a Hidden Gem Radar specifically surfaces highly-rated independent films with lower vote counts to prevent blockbuster bias, while a Plotly 3D network graph allows users to visually explore cinematic relationships.

projects.name: BoonSonClon Premium Furniture Store
projects.description: A high-performance full-stack e-commerce platform designed for premium furniture retail, featuring storefront browsing, product filtering, cart and order flows, admin product management, order inspection, member management, contact forms, and responsive UI pages.
projects.associated: Faculty of Information and Communication Technology, Mahidol University
highlights: Fully responsive customer and admin pages
projects.fullDescription:
[image: https://i.postimg.cc/c4T5THY2/Screenshot-2026-06-27-172948.png]

BoonSonClon is a high-performance, full-stack e-commerce platform designed exclusively for premium furniture retail, developed as the final project for the ITCS223 Web Development course. 

[media: 2]

The platform features a robust Hybrid Database System compatible with both PostgreSQL and SQLite, and a fully responsive Mobile-First card-based layout optimized for all viewports from mobile devices to desktop monitors.\n\nThe system is built with a clear separation of concerns, featuring a dedicated RESTful web service API and a polished storefront frontend. 

[media: 3]

It supports advanced e-commerce features, including clean server-side URL routing, dynamic product grids, and a comprehensive Admin Panel with secure JWT and session-based authentication for order and inventory management. The platform is designed for a seamless developer experience, featuring fully automated database seeding upon initialization, ensuring rapid deployment and testing environments.

[media: 7]

projects.name: Personal Home Lab Server
projects.description: A real compact home lab server rack running a Proxmox VE 9.2.3 cluster with LXC containers and virtual machines for networking, self-hosting, storage, automation, media, AI experiments, and infrastructure monitoring.
projects.fullDescription:
Building a Personal Home Lab Server is an ongoing journey of creating a private, self-hosted cloud environment from real hardware. The setup is built around a compact 10-inch rack with managed switching, structured patch cabling, mini PC hardware, and HP server hardware, giving the lab a practical footprint while still supporting multiple always-on services.

[media: 2]


The core of the infrastructure is powered by Proxmox VE 9.2.3. The cluster currently uses pve1 and pve2 nodes, with lightweight LXC containers and virtual machines for services such as AdGuard, InfluxDB, Grafana, Immich, Jellyfin, Ollama, Seerr, Radarr, Prowlarr, Open WebUI, Hermes Agent, Home Assistant, Ubuntu, and other automation or media workloads.

[media: 6]



Networking and security are handled with a self-hosted network stack and Tailscale-enabled remote access. This keeps services reachable through an encrypted private network while reducing the need to expose home infrastructure directly to the public internet.

The lab serves as a learning and production-style environment for system administration, virtualization, Docker-based services, monitoring, media hosting, local AI experimentation, storage, and infrastructure operations. It connects physical hardware design with practical DevOps workflows and real service maintenance.
highlights: Built a real compact rack with managed switching
Runs a Proxmox VE 9.2.3 cluster with pve1 and pve2 nodes
Hosts LXC containers and VMs for AdGuard

projects.name: BF Connect Android Application
projects.description: An Android application for breastfeeding support and clinical follow-up that connects mothers, nurses, and administrators through role-based dashboards, feeding and pumping logs, consultations, community moderation, knowledge content, and Firebase-backed data sync.
projects.fullDescription:
BF Connect is a production-oriented Android application for breastfeeding support, care coordination, and clinical follow-up. The app is designed around three primary roles: mothers, nurses, and administrators, each with dedicated workflows and dashboards.

[media: 2]

For mothers, the application supports profile management, breastfeeding and pumping logs, goal tracking, EPDS mental-health screening, consultation requests, community questions, and educational content. For nurses, it provides patient monitoring views, mother detail pages, consultation replies, community moderation, schedule support, and case follow-up. For administrators, it includes nurse approval, knowledge/news/quiz management, reporting, notifications, role oversight, and system setup.

[media: 4]


The backend is configured with Firebase Authentication, Google sign-in, Cloud Firestore, Firebase Storage for images/PDFs/videos, Firebase Cloud Messaging for role notifications, Firebase Crashlytics, Firebase Remote Config, and Cloud Functions v2. The project also includes production launch documentation, Play Store build notes, data-safety drafts, and Firebase deployment rules.
highlights: Breastfeeding and pumping log workflows with clinical follow-up support

projects.name: Shapr3D Custom Server Rack Mount
projects.description: A custom 3D-modeled server rack mount designed in Shapr3D for a compact 10-inch homelab rack setup. The design translates real product reference dimensions into a practical rack-mounted frame concept for mini PC and motherboard/server hardware mounting.
projects.fullDescription:
This project documents my custom 3D CAD design workflow for a compact server rack mounting component, modeled in Shapr3D as part of my personal home lab infrastructure work.

[media: 3]


The design process started from practical hardware requirements: adapting a compact mini PC / motherboard-style server setup into a clean 10-inch rack form factor. I used reference dimensions from rack-mount accessories and translated them into a custom frame with measured spacing, panel cutouts, side supports, screw holes, and structural geometry.

[media: 1]



The model focuses on functional hardware design rather than only visual rendering. The rack frame includes measured spans around 237 mm, mounting holes, front-panel clearance, and reinforced side geometry so the concept can be evaluated for fit, airflow, and physical installation inside a small server rack.

This project shows my ability to connect software engineering, networking infrastructure, and physical product design: planning server hardware, studying rack dimensions, designing CAD geometry, and iterating toward a usable homelab mounting solution.
highlights: Modeled a custom 10-inch server rack mounting frame in Shapr3D
Translated real product reference dimensions into CAD geometry
Connected homelab infrastructure planning with practical hardware design
